definitions.description: Run custom JavaScript or Python code

builderHint.searchHint: Use Code node as a LAST RESORT — it runs in a sandboxed environment and is slower than native nodes. Code node is ONLY appropriate for complex multi-step algorithms that cannot be expressed in single expressions, or operations requiring complex data structures. The sandbox has NO network access: fetch(), axios, XMLHttpRequest and require of http modules are unavailable and FAIL at runtime. NEVER make HTTP requests in a Code node — use the HTTP Request node and process its output instead.

relatedNodes.relationHint: Use this instead for ANY HTTP/API call — the Code node sandbox cannot make network requests
Use this instead for data manipulation: add/modify/rename fields, set values, map data
Use this instead for filtering items by condition
Use this instead for routing by condition
Use this instead for multi-way routing by condition
Use this instead for splitting arrays into separate items
Use this instead for combining multiple items into one
Use this instead for summarizing or pivoting data
Use this instead for removing duplicates
Use this instead to reduce the number of items returned
Use this instead for merging data from multiple branches
Use this instead for date time operations
Use this instead for creating html pages

extraTypeDefContent.content:
<patterns>
<pattern title="runOnceForAllItems with $input.all()">
const codeNode = node({
  type: 'n8n-nodes-base.code',
  version: 2,
  config: {
    name: 'Process Data',
    parameters: {
      mode: 'runOnceForAllItems',
      jsCode: `
const items = $input.all();
return items.map(item => ({
  json: { ...item.json, processed: true }
}));
`.trim()
    }
  }
});
</pattern>
</patterns>

options.name: Run Once for All Items
options.description: Run this code only once, no matter how many input items there are

options.name: Run Once for Each Item
options.description: Run this code as many times as there are input items

properties.name: jsCode
properties.description:
JavaScript code to execute.

Tip: You can use luxon vars like $today for dates and $jmespath for querying JSON structures. Learn more.

builderHint.propertyHint: The sandbox has NO network access: fetch(), axios, XMLHttpRequest and require of http modules are unavailable and fail at runtime. NEVER make HTTP requests here — use the HTTP Request node and process its output in this node instead.

properties.displayName: Type $ for a list of special vars/methods. Debug by using console.log() statements and viewing their output in the browser console.
properties.name: notice
pythonCode
properties.description:
Python code to execute.

Tip: You can use built-in methods and variables like _today for dates and _jmespath for querying JSON structures. Learn more.

builderHint.propertyHint: The sandbox has NO network access: requests, urllib, httpx and other HTTP libraries are unavailable and fail at runtime. NEVER make HTTP requests here — use the HTTP Request node and process its output in this node instead.

properties.name: pythonCode
properties.description:
Python code to execute.

Tip: You can use built-in methods and variables like _today for dates and _jmespath for querying JSON structures. Learn more.

builderHint.propertyHint: The sandbox has NO network access: requests, urllib, httpx and other HTTP libraries are unavailable and fail at runtime. NEVER make HTTP requests here — use the HTTP Request node and process its output in this node instead.

properties.displayName:
Debug by using print() statements and viewing their output in the browser console.

The Python option does not support _ syntax and helpers, except for _items in all-items mode and _item in per-item mode.
properties.name: notice
